quiz.title: Unit 12 Quiz 1, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Properties of Gases and Gas Laws Introduction
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Gas Properties, Kinetic Molecular Theory, Pressure, STP, Molar Volume, Diffusion

questions.question: Which statement about gases is TRUE?
options: Gases have definite shape and volume
Gas density is always greater than liquid density
questions.explanation: Gases are highly compressible due to the large empty spaces between particles. Gases have no definite shape or volume, particles are in constant motion, and gas density is much lower than liquid density.

questions.question: Gas pressure is caused by:
options: Collisions of gas particles with container walls
Repulsion between gas and container material
questions.explanation: Gas pressure results from the collisions of gas particles with the walls of their container. The force per unit area from these collisions is what we measure as pressure.

questions.question: Which gas would diffuse fastest at the same temperature?
questions.explanation: According to Graham's law of diffusion, the rate of diffusion is inversely proportional to the square root of molar mass. H₂ has the smallest molar mass (2 g/mol) among these options, so it will diffuse the fastest. O₂ (32 g/mol), N₂ (28 g/mol), and CO₂ (44 g/mol) are all heavier and diffuse more slowly.

questions.question: One of the following is FALSE
options: Gases have empty spaces between particles
Gases have relatively high kinetic energy
Gases have lower densities than air
questions.explanation: The statement 'Gases have lower densities than air' is false because not all gases have lower densities than air. Some gases, like CO₂, O₂, and N₂, have densities similar to or higher than air. Hydrogen and helium have lower densities than air, but this is not a universal property of all gases. The other statements are true according to kinetic molecular theory: gases have empty spaces between particles, high kinetic energy, and weak intermolecular forces.

questions.question: Standard temperature and pressure (STP) is defined as:
questions.explanation: STP is defined as 0°C (273.15 K) and 1 atm pressure. Option D is equivalent (760 torr = 1 atm), but option A is the standard definition. 25°C is room temperature, and 0 K is absolute zero.

questions.question: At STP, one mole of any ideal gas occupies:
questions.explanation: At STP (0°C, 1 atm), one mole of any ideal gas occupies 22.4 L. This is known as the molar volume of a gas at STP and is independent of the gas identity.

questions.question: All the following are properties of Gases except:
questions.explanation: Osmosis is the movement of solvent molecules through a semipermeable membrane and is a property of solutions, not gases. Diffusion, elastic collisions, and Brownian motion are all properties of gases.

questions.question: Which unit is commonly used to measure gas pressure?
questions.explanation: Atmosphere (atm) is a common unit of pressure. Other pressure units include torr, mmHg, Pa, and bar. Joule is energy, Kelvin is temperature, and mole is amount of substance.

questions.question: Which gas property explains why smells spread across a room?
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the process by which gas particles spread from areas of high concentration to low concentration. This explains why odors can be detected throughout a room.

questions.question: Which property allows a gas to be transferred easily through a tube?
questions.explanation: Fluidity is the ability of a substance to flow. Gases, like liquids, are fluids and can flow through tubes and pipes easily due to their low viscosity.

questions.question: Which statement about gas particles is TRUE?
options: They move in straight lines until collision
With or without collision, they move randomly
questions.explanation: According to the kinetic molecular theory, gas particles move in straight lines until they collide with other particles or the container walls. They do not vibrate in place (solids do), and they do not have fixed spacing.

questions.question: Which factor increases the rate of diffusion of a gas?
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases the kinetic energy of gas particles, causing them to move faster and diffuse more rapidly. Lower temperature and larger molar mass decrease diffusion rate (Graham's law).

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the basic properties of gases! 🧪⚗️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the kinetic molecular theory and gas properties.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on understanding pressure, STP, and diffusion.
scoreMessages.0: Please review gas properties, kinetic molecular theory, and basic gas terminology.
